claim: Write code incrementally in small chunks with validation at each step, rather than generating large monolithic code blocks, to make debugging easier for both humans and AI models
actionable: Break code generation into small incremental steps with validation/testing between each step instead of asking for full implementations
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Multi-layered horizontal code generation (database -> services -> API -> frontend) creates a large debugging surface that's hard for both humans and AI to reason about
actionable: Avoid asking AI to generate complete horizontal stacks at once; instead, build vertical slices that work end-to-end
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Use structured planning (questions, research, design, structure, plan, implement) with AI to improve both human-AI and human-human alignment on complex features
actionable: Create high-level markdown plans with AI first before implementing, spot-check plans but save detailed review for actual code
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Without AI, a 2-hour coding feature often takes 2+ days due to planning, alignment, code review, and rework cycles across teams
actionable: Use AI planning to compress planning and alignment phases, which can be more valuable than raw coding speedup
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: AI is shifting society from a knowledge-scarcity economy to a knowledge-abundance economy, changing the value proposition of knowledge workers
actionable: Evaluate your professional value on execution and judgment rather than knowledge hoarding, as AI commoditizes knowledge
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: AI enables non-developers (PMs, domain experts) to write code at developer-level quality, meaning traditional coding skills are no longer the primary differentiator
actionable: If your value comes solely from typing in an IDE, invest in higher-level skills like system design, judgment, and domain expertise
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: When working on large projects that split into discrete independent pieces, spinning up parallel AI agents for each piece can be more efficient than sequential processing
actionable: For large features with independent components, create separate agents working in parallel rather than having one agent do everything sequentially
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: The main bottleneck in AI-assisted development is not code generation speed but evaluating whether the product is actually good and humans will want to use it
actionable: After generating code with AI, spend time testing the actual user experience and iterating based on real human feedback
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Testing and verification with AI agents remains an unsolved problem that requires further innovation
actionable: Be cautious about relying on AI to verify its own output without human oversight; implement robust testing strategies
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: LangGraph provides workflow orchestration for stateful agents that can resume from where they left off without rerunning previous steps
actionable: Use LangGraph to maintain agent state across long workflows, allowing agents to resume and avoid re-executing completed validation steps
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Combine deterministic workflow steps with non-deterministic LLM calls at specific stages to balance structure with flexibility in agent systems
actionable: Design agent workflows with fixed control flow steps, but let LLMs handle the reasoning within each step
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Don't give agents full autonomy by default; carefully consider where agent autonomy actually helps vs. where structure and constraints are needed
actionable: For each part of your workflow, explicitly decide if it needs agent freedom or if a constrained, deterministic step is better
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!

claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) provides a standardized way to connect agents with external tools and data sources
actionable: Use MCP servers to provide consistent interfaces for agents to access external tools and data in agentic workflows
source_title: AIE Miami Keynote & Talks ft. OpenCode. Google Deepmind, OpenAI, and more!
